comparisonSemantics.verdictNormalizedSha256: The activated theorem verdict fields match after normalization.
comparisonSemantics.activatedStatusNormalizedSha256: The activated status coordinates and premise flags match after normalization.
comparisonSemantics.proofScriptOutputsNormalizedSha256: Focused proof-script accept outputs match after normalization.
comparisonSemantics.artifactsOrLogsNormalizedSha256: Log-like evidence matches after timestamp, ANSI, line-ending, blank-line, and trailing-whitespace normalization.
comparisonSemantics.runRecordNormalizedSha256: The full normalized run record matches, including runner metadata and evidence references.

nonClaims: Digest comparison is reproducibility metadata for activated checker-trust verifier runs.
A digest match is not an external-consensus claim or peer-review acceptance.
A digest mismatch is a triage signal; it does not by itself refute the source/checker proof stack.
The source/checker verifier remains the proof-stack reproduction target.
